lines.text: [REDACTED] to DeLoach
Re: [REDACTED]
By letter dated 9/17/63, [REDACTED] advised he had been interviewed on that
date by SA [REDACTED] (Senior Resident Agent, Newport News, Virginia, assigned Norfolk
Office, EOD 2/2/48, GS-13) regarding [REDACTED] interest in reports of sightings of UFO
According to [REDACTED] stated he was contacting him in connection with his
inquiries and that he intended to prepare "a memorandum for the files." [REDACTED]
requested a copy of SA [REDACTED] memorandum. Prior to responding to [REDACTED] the
Norfolk Office advised as follows: [REDACTED] has directed a voluminous amount of corre-
spondence to military officials in the area and as [REDACTED] was employed by the Departm
of the Army, Fort Eustis, Virginia, the G-2 Section of the Second Army, Fort Meade,
Maryland, was conducting a background check of [REDACTED] The Norfolk Office
was in receipt of several letters directed to other government agencies which were
subsequently forwarded to the Norfolk Office. This included a letter to the Commandin
Officer, Headquarters, Aircraft Fleet, Atlantic U. S. Naval Base, Norfolk, Virginia,
6/15/60; Commanding Officer, Cape Charles Air Force Station, Cape Charles, Virgini
8/7/58; Commander, U. S. Naval Base, Norfolk, Virginia, 8/8/63; [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] postmarked Fort Eustis, Virginia, 7/23/63.
[REDACTED] was interviewed on 9/7/63 by SA [REDACTED] in order to obtain back-
ground information concerning him in order to reply to the correspondence from the
[REDACTED] and the U. S. Naval Base, Norfolk, Virginia, mentioned
above. At the conclusion of the interview, [REDACTED] wanted to know what would be done
about the information he furnished SA [REDACTED] at which time he was told that the infor-
mation he furnished would be made a matter of record in FBI files.
[REDACTED] was subsequently advised by in-absence letter of 9/27/63 that the
confidential nature of our files would preclude complying with his request (the copy of S
[REDACTED] memorandum concerning his interview with [REDACTED]
It appears that the reference to the FBI on page 6 of [REDACTED] manuscript
is not completely true. However, it has the effect of leaving the wrong impression; the
FBI was not investigating [REDACTED] but was merely interviewing him in order to respond
to inquiries directed to us from other agencies. SA [REDACTED] did not interview [REDACTED]
merely for the purpose of writing "a memorandum for the files" but that his interview
of [REDACTED] would be made a matter of record in FBI files. Also, the phraseology used
by [REDACTED] regarding SA [REDACTED] reference to the confidential nature of FBI files could
be misleading. These points should be brought to the attention of the Department of
Defense.
RECOMMENDATION:
That [REDACTED] manuscript on UFO be returned to the Department of Defense
by our liaison representative with that agency at which time that agency can be informed
that the FBI does not agree with [REDACTED] portrayal of his interview by the FBI or his
misrepresentation of the Bureau's role in investigating of UFO. However, the Depart-
ment of Defense should not attribute these comments to the FBI and we are deferring
to their judgments as to authorizing publication of the article.
[unclear]
